participants.role: Principal Private Secretary to the Cabinet  Secretary

text:
Secretary, CO) and others 
Subject: RE: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input  
Date: Monday, 15 September 2025 at 18:57:30 British Summer Time  
From: Caroline Hurndall  
To: Ailsa Terry, 
, Simon Madden  
CC: Tom Bramley, Symes, Elkie - CO (OFF-SEN), Ian Collard *, Ellen Atkinson, Sean Cherry 
Attachments: image001.jpg  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
Thanks Ailsa – 
 has tidied up / proofed and this will go to the FS for clearance tonight. 
Assuming she’s happy, we’ll send first thing tomorrow morning. Mr Doughty will also draw on it 
for his SO24 speech (on which there is a separate chain).  
Thanks  
Caroline  
Caroline Hurndall │PPS/FS │ 
The Foreign Secretary’s box deadline is midday, Monday to Thursday. Please allow a minimum 
of 48hrs to process work sent to FSPO. Please also copy 
 to all 
submissions/updates to the Foreign Secretary.  
_______________________________________________________________________________ 
From: Ailsa Terry  
Sent: 15 September 2025 17:54  
To: 
 ; Simon Madden ; Caroline Hurndall  
Cc: Tom Bramley ; Symes, Elkie - CO (OFF-SEN) ; Ian Collard * ; Ellen 
Atkinson ; Sean Cherry Subject: RE: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson 
CO/UKSV input  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
Hi all 
I have added in tracked changes including a few typos – this can now go after a thorough proof 
read. I have suggested removing the reference to due diligence as open source material ref as 
a) I am not sure we need to add it in and b) it will make it harder to mount a defence to not
release as per the separate advice we received and are considering.
Ailsa
_______________________________________________________________________________ 
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE  
From: 
Sent: 15 September 2025 14:22  
To: Simon Madden 
; Ailsa Terry 
Cc: Caroline Hurndall 
; Tom 
Bramley

 Ian Collard *  
Ellen Atkinson 
Sean Cherry 
Subject: RE: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input  
Hi All,  
Many thanks for your comments. Please see attached an updated version of the letter, this 
takes onboard your comments and also final checks within FCDO.  
Ailsa – on your text for answer 4 to explain how we have handled:

reference mitigations.  
Line on no ministerial role included in the cover letter.  
Grateful if I can have final confirmation you are content (in particular No.10 and Cab Sec) and 
we will then put to the FS.  
We are still working to get the letter out asap today, so grateful for quick responses (the 
next FS window to review will be 1500, after that it will be 1600).  
Kind regards,  
 
| Deputy Private Secretary to the Permanent Under-Secretaries, Sir 
Oliver Robbins and Nick Dyer | Permanent Under-Secretaries’ Joint Private Office

On Mon, 15 Sep 2025 at 13:13, Ailsa Terry 
wrote: 
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE  
Dear Caroline 
We are content with the draft as we saw it although we would like to answer question 4 with 
something closer to ‘There are strict confidentiality rules around sensitive personal 
information in the vetting process and as a result we do not comment on individual National 
Security Vetting cases and any mitigations employed around specific individuals. These 
processes are conducted entirely independently of Ministers and findings/details other than 
the outcome are not shared with them’. But to ensure we are clearing the final doc, can you 
resend to this chain given there were further edits being made this morning?  
Personal

wants to see a version, suggest you show him the version I sent but with either comments of 
tracks in as you see fit.  
Caroline  
Caroline Hurndall │PPS/FS │

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE  
From: Tom Bramley 
Sent: 14 September 2025 20:42  
To: Elkie Symes 
Cc: Simon Madden 
; Caroline Hurndall 
 Ailsa Terry (No.10) 
; Ian Collard * 
Subject: Re: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input  
Thanks for sharing and sorry to chip in on this too.  
On National Security Vetting, I think it is worth being clear that while UKSV conduct the 
vetting checks and make a recommendation, vetting decisions are made by the sponsor 
department. Would suggest the following addition to the cover letter (and then repeated 
im the questions):  
"Separately from this there is a long-standing formal process for National Security Vetting 
which is undertaken independently by the UK Security Vetting Agency (hosted by the Cabinet 
Office) on behalf of individual departments and reporting back to them for decision."  
Tom  
Tom Bramley  
Director, Security Policy and Strategy

On Sun, 14 Sept 2025 at 20:19, Simon Madden 
wrote:  
Hi Caroline,  
Just to follow up on initial response from me.  
The references in the draft letter are ok but I’d prefer it to say that No10 asked us to do 
this. We don’t usually play a part in diplomatic appointments.  
On the Annex as I said just now it would be wrong to link our due diligence with security or 
vetting. So when you say FCDO didn’t play any part and no security concerns were raised, 
we would not expect any security concerns to be flagged as that was not the due diligence 
we were doing nor are we qualified to make security judgements. So if those references in 
the annex could be amended they would be great.  
As I said, we don’t want to give a misleading impression of our due dil 
exercise. Thanks  
Simon  
Simon Madden  
Director of Propriety & Ethics  
| 70 Whitehall | London | SW1A 2AS  
_______________________________________________________________________________ 
On Sun, 14 Sep 2025 at 20:04, Simon Madden 
 wrote:  
Hi I have a problem. I cannot agree to a reference to our DD process being linked to 
raising or not raising security concerns. That was not its purpose. That woujd be 
misleading.  
Our due diligence was on Mandelson generally using open source material. It was not 
a security vetting process and it offered no judgements. It was not specific to Epstein. 
This was in addition to usual pre-employment checks carried out by others.  
The text we had before was what we are content with  
Thanks

On Sun, 14 Sep 2025 at 19:50, Caroline Hurndall 
wrote: OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE  
Ailsa,  
With apologies for the delay, please see attached a draft response to the FAC 
letter of 12 Sept, which I’ve also re-attached here for ease.  
The FS has seen this but not yet cleared it for release – you’ll see there are a couple 
of areas that she wants triple checked, so that we can be totally confident that we are 
releasing accurate information to parliament. If there are suggested changes from 
No10 or Cabinet Office colleagues, please send them back to me so I can incorporate 
and clear with her.  
As per my earlier email, you’ll see we’ve refocussed the letter to be based on FCDO 
process, but drawn on the info sent over by PET and CabOffice colleagues in terms of 
process, for which many thanks.  
Best wishes and no doubt speak tomorrow,  
Caroline 
Caroline Hurndall │PPS/FS │ 
_______________________________________________________________________________

worked in before release this evening.  
Ailsa

On Sun, 14 Sep 2025 at 15:27, Simon Madden 
 wrote:  
Sorry to come back so late on this. Ellen and I have just discussed the reference to our due 
diligence. We’d like to amend so we lose the last sentence referring specifically to Epstein, as 
our commission was a general request for DD not a specific one on PM/JE So it should read:  
##  
In this case, No10 also asked the Propriety and Ethics team in the Cabinet Office to 
undertake some due diligence, based on open source material, alongside the usual pre 
employment checks.  
###  
We do not want to create the impression that we were specifically commissioned by No10 to 
look at that point. On second reading Ellen spotted this potential reading of the text and I agree 
too.  
Grateful for confirmation of the amendment.  
Thanks and apologies for the late intervention.  
Simon  
Simon Madden  
Director of Propriety & Ethics  
 70 Whitehall |London | SW1A 2AS  
_______________________________________________________________________________

On Sun, 14 Sept 2025 at 11:10, Vincent Devine 
 wrote:  
Elkie,  
This appears to be based on a misunderstanding of how the security vetting process 
operates in FCDO. I think Tom and UKSV colleagues have set that out for otehrs over the 
last few days.  
Let me see if I can raise the FCO security leads and check if they have been involved in 
these latest discussions  
Regardless of whether CO, UKSV or FCDO attempt to answer these questions, we are 
unlikely to be able to give any meaningful responses to Qs 1 to 4 without taking legal advice. 
 
Vincent  
Vincent Devine  
Director General & Government Chief Security O]icer  
Government Security Function  
  
Chief of Staff 
  
  
  
  
Private Secretary

On Sun, 14 Sep 2025 at 10:08, 
 
 wrote: 
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE  
--- Resending to correct email ----  
Elkie,  
Grateful if you can commission CO/UKSV to input into an Annex that will be shared with the 
FAC in response to their attached letter re. Mandelson. Grateful if we can have input by 2pm 
today please as we are aiming for FS to issue the letter today.  
Given FCDO do not own the security clearance process, the Foreign Secretary will not want 
to take responsibility for responding to the FAC on these matters. We therefore propose the 
FS write a short cover letter and an annex (owned by the Cabinet Office) with responses to 
their questions.  
To support your teams, the questions are set out below and I have included where we think 
the FCDO does have agency to answer the questions are they refer specifically to FCDO/the 
FS.  
Annex A - Cabinet Office response to the FAC questions 
1. What security concerns were raised by the agencies undertaking security clearance ahead
of Lord Mandelson’s appointment? [For CO to answer]
2. What was the response of the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office to any
such concerns? FCDO advice was not sought, nor given.
3. Was it the assessment of the FCDO that any such concerns represented a potential barrier
to Lord Mandelson’s appointment? FCDO advice was
not sought, nor given.  
4. Were any conditions imposed on the remit of Lord Mandelson as Ambassador in
order to mitigate any security concerns? [For CO to answer]
5. Was there a decision made to dismiss security concerns? Was such a
decision taken by the FCDO or by No. 10? If so, by whom? [For CO to answer]
6. If no decision was taken to straightforwardly dismiss security concerns raised by the
relevant agencies, was a decision made to suspend or alter: (a) the usual security
vetting requirements; or, (b) the usual timeline for vetting procedures? [For CO to
answer]
7. Was it the assessment of the Foreign Secretary that there was sufficient time
between the announcement of Lord Mandelson’s appointment in December and the
beginning of his tenure on 10 February to carry out full thorough vetting? Yes. While an
expedited clearance process is faster than a routine clearance, it is not unusual to do
so, and within the bounds of the vetting process.
8. Did the usual vetting process for diplomatic appointments commence in
relation to Lord Mandelson before his appointment was made public? [For
CO to answer]
Kind regards, 
 
| Deputy Private Secretary to the Permanent Under  
Secretaries, Sir Oliver Robbins and Nick Dyer | Permanent Under-Secretaries’ Joint 
Personal

JCS
